mnemonics: Blocks are CHAINed together using cryptography to form a secure, public LEDGER
keyPoints: Decentralization: No single entity controls the data
Immutability: Records cannot be altered once added
Transparency: All transactions are visible to network participants
commonMistakes: Blockchain is Bitcoin (Bitcoin uses blockchain tech, but blockchain has many other uses)
All data on a blockchain is private (It is often cryptographically secure but transparent)
Blockchains are always slow and inefficient (Newer consensus mechanisms are addressing this)
